activated_rules.rule_name: Certificate invoice reference matched an invoice in the separate invoice file
Consignor found explicitly in invoice text outside the main issuer heading
At least one of multiple certificate quantities verified against invoice
Box 6 shipment/item quantities considered before any quantity mismatch

consignor.motivation: Certificate consignor 'Volvo Merchandise AB' is explicitly present in the invoice text on page 1 in the seller/footer identification block together with Swedish address details and country 'Sweden'. Under rule 4.1.0 this is sufficient even though the invoice heading is branded 'VOLVO'. Country matches exactly as Sweden. No unsupported corporate assumption was needed.

consignee.motivation: The certificate consignee name 'Volvo Group Korea Ltd' appears explicitly in the invoice in both the Invoicing address and Delivery address blocks. The country on the certificate is 'Korea, Republic of' and the invoice states 'Korea'; this is the same country after allowed country-name normalization. Address detail differences do not prevent a match under rule 4.2.4.

113066 Short Winter Jacket 1 99 M 6201401000 CN 7
113066 Short Winter Jacket 1 99 L 6201401000 CN 4
113470 Iron Mark Pin (10-pack) 2 8310000000 CN 13
goods_description.motivation: Article numbers are present in the certificate, so primary verification was performed by exact article-number matching under rule 4.3.2. Certificate article numbers 113066 and 113470 are explicitly present in the invoice, and the accompanying descriptions correspond to the same items. The minor formatting difference '10 pack' vs '10-pack' does not change the result because the article number match already verifies the goods.

quantity.motivation: The certificate contains multiple quantity expressions: shipment/item quantities in box 6 and '14,69 KG NET' in box 7. Under rules 4.4.3.1 and 4.4.3.2, it is sufficient to verify at least one certificate quantity before concluding mismatch. The invoice explicitly shows the same item quantities 7, 4 and 13 for the corresponding goods. No explicit invoice total net-weight value contradicts the certificate's 14,69 KG NET. The result is therefore MATCH without relying on an unpermitted calculated total weight.

Item num. Description Conf. Colour Size HS Code CoO
...
113066 ... CN

113470 ... CN
origin.motivation: The certificate states country of origin 'CN'. The invoice explicitly shows 'CoO' with value 'CN' for each listed item line. This is a direct and explicit origin statement in the invoice, so the certificate origin is positively verified under rules 4.5.1 and 4.5.5.

overall_assessment.decision_summary: The document pair was validly matched through certificate reference 'Invoice 776069' and the separate invoice file shows invoice 776069. Consignor was explicitly identifiable in the invoice text, consignee matched the invoice address blocks, goods matched by exact article numbers, certificate quantities were sufficiently verified through the explicit line quantities 7/4/13 under rules 4.4.3.1 and 4.4.3.2, and origin 'CN' was explicitly stated in the invoice CoO column. No LC exception applied and no applicable control point resulted in MISMATCH or MANUAL_REVIEW.
